topic: A person learns their apartment building is going condo
raw_output:
[story 1]

The glossy brochure had been tucked under the mail, a sleek, impersonal invitation to a life of elevated living. I picked it up again, tracing the sharp edges of the paper with a fingertip, my breath catching in my throat. It detailed the impending conversion of the entire complex into luxury condominiums. A slow, cold dread began to settle in my stomach, a heavy, nauseating weight. I looked around my cramped, familiar hallway, the peeling paint and the mismatched furniture suddenly feeling painfully inadequate, like a cheap costume in a high-fashion photoshoot. I mumbled something incoherent to myself, shoving the pamphlet back into the pile as if it were contaminated.

[story 2]

"So, the building is going condo?" I asked Sarah, my voice coming out higher than intended, a thin, reedy sound. I watched her reaction—a slight, almost imperceptible tightening around her eyes—and immediately felt a flush creep up my neck, warming my cheeks until they felt tight and hot. I avoided meeting her gaze, focusing instead on a scuff mark on the linoleum floor. Every glance at the apartment felt like being scrutinized under a harsh spotlight. I fiddled constantly with the loose thread on my sweater, my hands trembling just slightly as I tried to formulate a response that didn't betray the churning anxiety in my chest.

[story 3]

I stood by the window, staring out at the neighboring buildings, suddenly hyper-aware of my own small, unremarkable existence within the structure. The news had hit me like a physical blow—the transformation into exclusive units. My jaw tightened, and I unconsciously started smoothing down my already rumpled shirt, a futile attempt to project some semblance of composure. Every sound from the hallway seemed amplified, every distant conversation sounding like an accusation. I wanted to melt into the wall, to become invisible, because the prospect of being suddenly exposed, of having my current reality deemed insufficient, made my skin crawl with a desperate need to retreat.
